subject: Anthropology
title: Redistribution
description: A quick, practical guide to Redistribution.

hook.text: You give grain to the chief. The chief gives some back during famine. This isn't charity — it's redistribution.

buildup.text: Redistribution collects resources at a center — a chief, temple, or state — then reallocates them to the community.

discovery.text: It requires hierarchy. Someone must have authority to collect and distribute, creating a political economy.

twist.text: Modern taxes are redistribution. Welfare, schools, and roads are funded the same way ancient chiefs fed their people.

climax.text: Redistribution links economics to power. Whoever controls the flow of resources controls the community.

punchline.text: Central authority was born from redistribution.

quiz.question: What is redistribution?
options: Collecting resources centrally and reallocating them to the community
